Loss Context: 76 rating

What it means: Justin Gaethje's Loss Context combines worst-loss severity, counted loss burden per UFC fight through prime, a minimum burden for repeated prime losses, and strong-division relief. The live penalty is -3.93.

Final Loss Context
-3.93
Worst-loss severity
2.25 from the two worst counted losses
Loss frequency
2.37 across 13 UFC fights through prime
Prime-loss floor
3.00 · not needed
Division relief
15% · 0.69 points saved
Post-prime treatment
Post-prime fights and no contests are excluded.
Why Ranked Here

Gaethje ranks here because the UFC-only case now has real championship weight: undisputed lightweight gold, two interim/title-level wins, and a modern lightweight win list built around Topuria, Ferguson, Poirier, Chandler, Fiziev, Barboza, Cerrone, and Johnson.

Why Not Ranked Higher?

He does not rank higher because the loss context is still heavy after the context adjustment. Gaethje has been finished in major prime fights, and one undisputed title win does not erase the Khabib, Oliveira, Max, Poirier, and Alvarez damage against cleaner all-time cases.

        Justin Gaethje wins this comparison.
        Dricus keeps debates interesting because the high-end wins are stronger than his total UFC time would suggest.Gaethje wins comparisons when modern lightweight strength, action-fight résumé, high-end win quality, and undisputed title value matter more than clean title reign length.Dricus du Plessis may win one version of the argument, but Justin Gaethje wins the full ranking comparison.
        Final take: Justin Gaethje wins, barely. Dricus du Plessis has the strongest counterargument, but Justin Gaethje's total resume gives him the edge.
